input.initialPrompt:
I have an HTTP-based C# MCP server built with ASP.NET Core.
How do I write integration tests that test the full MCP protocol
over HTTP without spinning up a real server?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to write integration tests for a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server built with ASP.NET Core, testing the full MCP protocol over HTTP without spinning up a real server. This is a knowledge/guidance question, so I can answer directly without needing to explore a codebase.

The standard approach for ASP.NET Core integration testing without a real server is to use `WebApplicationFactory<T>` from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`, which creates an in-memory test server. Combined with an MCP client that can use a custom `HttpClient`, this lets you test the full protocol stack.


I'll walk through the practical setup and show how to structure these tests effectively.
